name: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with amorphous chitin (PMC7443923)
description: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with amorphous chitin — medium reported in PMC7443923 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC7443923 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: The 'Laguna Chitin' mineral medium (LC medium) was prepared on the basis of ONR7a medium (45), modified by the addition of (final concentration, g l-1): 200 NaCl; 30 MgCl2; 0.5 NH4Cl and 5 HEPES
provenance.base_medium: ONR7a artificial seawater medium (Dyksterhouse et al. 1995; DSMZ Medium 950), made hypersaline (240 g/L total salinity) by raising NaCl to 200 g/L and MgCl2 to 30 g/L, buffered with 5 g/L HEPES at pH 7.0, plus Pfennig & Lippert (1966) acidic trace-metal solution and vitamin mix, 50 mg/L yeast extract, and amorphous chitin as carbon source.

sources_chased.what: paper main text (PMC7443923) - LC salinity, Na+/Mg2+, chitin
SI Appendix (pnas.2007232117.sapp.pdf) - full LC recipe & base-medium citation
added: NaCl 200 / MgCl2 30 / NH4Cl 0.5 / HEPES 5 g/L modifications

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 45 (Dyksterhouse 1995, ONR7a medium)
trace-metal + vitamin stocks cited as ref 46 (Pfennig & Lippert 1966)
sources_chased.how: DSMZ MediaDive solutions S3087 (trace) and S3031 (vitamins)

missing_before: sulfate/phosphate/K+/Ca2+ were present but underspecified; NaBr, NaF, SrCl2, EDTA, buffers (HEPES/TAPSO) newly enumerated
recuration.notes: LC medium is a hypersaline (240 g/L) derivative of ONR7a: base ONR7a salts + NaCl to 200 g/L, MgCl2 to 30 g/L, NH4Cl 0.5 g/L, HEPES buffer to pH 7.0, plus 1 mL/L each of the Pfennig & Lippert (1966) acidic trace-metal solution and vitamin mix, 50 mg/L yeast extract, and amorphous chitin (hydrolyzed extracellularly to GlcNAc) as sole carbon source. No chemical reductant is added; the host is aerobic/facultative and the coculture self-generates microoxic conditions in sealed serum bottles (hence oxygen=facultative, microoxic optimum). Trace/vitamin per-L amounts are the DSMZ rendering of the cit

yeast extract.citation: Yeast extract = autolysate of Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Component amounts here are QUANTITATIVE (mg per g yeast extract), from Tao Z et al., J Microbiol Biotechnol 2022;32:1236-1247, Table 2 (doi:10.4014/jmb.2207.07057, PMC9998214): free amino acids ~35% w/w with the measured pattern (Ala/Glu/Asp/Leu/Arg/Lys dominant; Cys/Met/Trp minor), B-vitamins (niacin >> pyridoxine/pantothenate > thiamine > riboflavin/folate/B12; biotin trace), ~10% RNA-derived nucleosides, and minerals (K/P-rich). Corroborated by Podpora B et al., Czech J Food Sci 2016;34:554-563, doi:10.17221/419/2015-CJFS; Atlas RM, Handbook of Microbiological Media, doi:10.1201/EBK1439804063. Per-component lower bounds are scaled to molar abundance (mg/g ÷ molecular weight).

components.decomposition_ref: Yeast extract = autolysate of Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Component amounts here are QUANTITATIVE (mg per g yeast extract), from Tao Z et al., J Microbiol Biotechnol 2022;32:1236-1247, Table 2 (doi:10.4014/jmb.2207.07057, PMC9998214): free amino acids ~35% w/w with the measured pattern (Ala/Glu/Asp/Leu/Arg/Lys dominant; Cys/Met/Trp minor), B-vitamins (niacin >> pyridoxine/pantothenate > thiamine > riboflavin/folate/B12; biotin trace), ~10% RNA-derived nucleosides, and minerals (K/P-rich). Corroborated by Podpora B et al., Czech J Food Sci 2016;34:554-563, doi:10.17221/419/2015-CJFS; Atlas RM, Handbook of Microbiological Media, doi:10.1201/EBK1439804063. Per-component lower bounds are scaled to molar abundance (mg/g ÷ molecular weight).
